questions.question: A rectangular prism has length 5 cm, width 3 cm, and height 4 cm. What is its volume?
questions.explanation: Volume of a prism = length × width × height = 5 × 3 × 4 = 60 cm³.

questions.question: A cylinder has a radius of 2 meters and a height of 10 meters. What is its volume in terms of π?
questions.explanation: Volume of a cylinder = πr²h = π(2)²(10) = π(4)(10) = 40π m³.

questions.question: A sphere has a radius of 3 inches. What is its volume in terms of π?
A cone has a base radius of 5 cm and a height of 6 cm. What is its volume in terms of π?
A triangular prism has a triangular base with area 12 square meters and a height (depth) of 8 meters. What is the volume of the prism?
questions.explanation: Volume of a prism = base area × height = 12 × 8 = 96 m³.

questions.question: A cylinder has a volume of 180π cubic inches and a height of 5 inches. What is the radius of the cylinder?
questions.explanation: Volume = πr²h, so 180π = πr²(5). Divide by 5π: r² = 36, so r = 6 inches.
